full_text:
**Project 9 - EyeMT**

*Multiomics (tCycIF 'proteomics', GeoMx spatial transcriptomics on the
adjacent slide and WES genomics) data integration for understanding the
role of different immune cell types (mainly T-cells and myeloid cells)
and interactions between them in shaping cancer immune escape mechanisms
and the overall outcome of the patients*

**Responsible personnel:**

*Responsible personnel:*

- *Iga Niemiec (05.2023 -)*

- *Aleksandra Shabanova (08.2024 -)*

- *Saundarya Shah (CycIF experiments) (2023-)*

- *Zhihan Liang (geomx experiments) (08.2024-)*

- *Gayani Anandagoda (ligand-receptor analysis of geomx
 > data)(11.2024-05.2025)*

- *Elias Ruuska (CycIF QC and spatial analysis) (04.2025-)*

**Input files used:**

[Description]{.underline}:

> *1. DCC GeoMx spatial transcriptomics files*

*2. ome.tiff tCycIF multiplex images*

*3. results of various downstream analysis of WES data from iCAN
platform*

[Paths:]{.underline}

1. h30492/farkkilab2/Data/geomx/dcc

2. h30492/farkkilab2/Data/cycif/batch(1)(2)(3)adjacent*\_*slides/tif

3. h30492/farkkilab2/Data/extra_datasets/ican

Batch 1 (Inga-Maria's paper)

**Main experimental and data analysis plan:**

1. GeoMx experiment in 3 batches (08.2023 + 11.2024 + 03.2025)

2. lowplex tCycIF for adjacent slides in 3 batches (05.2023 + 11.2024 +
 > 03.2025)

3. processing of GeoMx data (03-05.2025)

4. processing of tCycIF data (08.2024-12.2025)

5. downstream analysis of all data (01-03.2026)

6. combining data into statistical/ml model (01-03.2026)

## 11.12.2025

*Meeting with Anniina*

*Presentation:* [*[EyeMT:

n]{.underline}](https://docs.google.com/document/d/155Ln7pe43QiRyS8TzYoQQeWAG3fY7riuTOPXT_pacqQ/edit?usp=sharing)

## 11.12.2025

*Meeting with Anniina*

*Presentation:* [*[EyeMT:
updates]{.underline}*](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/166ks3RbPhePkR7y4mk3eM4Qe4izT6lHsFkojmWrAaSc/edit?usp=sharing)

Notes

A. Cell Phenotyping Strategy

- Batch 3: Use the CD20 marker.

- Merge CD11c and Iba1 populations. (maybe T cells as well) -\>
 > Action: check how SPACEStat is affected by merged phenotypes

- Check Tribus heatmap of marker intensities across different cell
 > types as QC along with Umap

B. ROI Integration Principles

- For integration analysis, interaction hubs and ROIs should be at the
 > same scale -\> Action: SPACEStat tweaking?

- The threshold for network interaction must be a tradeoff between the
 > resulting cell count and the number of hubs, optimizing for
 > downstream statistical analysis.

- Collect ROIs that do not overlap with network interaction hubs and
 > check them visually. In theory, these could serve as negative
 > controls to investigate background activity.

community labels

## 19.05.2026

Meeting with Anniina

1. Area and DAPI ratio qc

Be lenient with threshold as staining of DAPI is changing

2. Choose chromosomes of interest in genomic table

3. Component and communities

Could proceed first with matching logic for ROI selection, GeoMx
labels - at least two cell groups/components of distinct cell types

Focus - myeloid - T cell interactions

Drop B cells for tCycIF

Cell of interest - CD4, CD8 T cell and Myeloid

- Two ROIs missing coordinates ?

- Communities definition

1. In simulations, we get higher number of single-cell components for
 > Myeloid -\> sample with high number of Myeloid in general -\>
 > Elias updated the distance threshold for Myeloid.

2. The definition of communities with at least 2 single-cell components
 > of distinct cell types resulted in 100-500 cell counts (similar to
 > ROIs cell counts).

3. Cluster labels for communities - compare single cell composition and

ell components

 > between outlines

5. Compare new definition of communities with ROIs
 > [[niemiec.iga@gmail.com]{.underline}](mailto:niemiec.iga@gmail.com)

Approaches to integrate:

1. Communities matching ROIs

2. Defining single-cell components -\> window of ROI -\> composition of
 > single-cell components along with top immune-related pathways -\>
 > more refined clusters based on spatial and molecular programs

3. Use either communities' labels or ROIs
